sanskrit:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम्।
ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना॥
transliteration:
brahmārpaṇaṃ brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam |
brahmaiva tena gantavyaṃ brahma-karma-samādhinā ||
literal_meaning: The ladle is Brahman, the oblation is Brahman, the fire is Brahman, the offerer is Brahman; thus absorbed in Brahman-action, one attains Brahman alone.
contextual_meaning: Every element of action—tool, material, fire, doer—dissolves into one conscious field; when identity merges with this field, anxiety-driven doing becomes self-liberating play.
psychological_state: Flow-state/samādhi: subject–object boundary thins; performance feels intrinsically meaningful and self-validating.
trigger_scenario: A coder in 'the zone' suddenly realizes the code, the screen, the typing fingers and the intention are one continuous process—bug-fixing becomes joyful rather than stressful.
anchor_text: See every part of action as pure awareness and act as that.
